summary: Renamed and decompiled many fighter item-pickup callbacks as explicit ft<Character>_OnItemPickup functions, updating ftdata callback tables to use semantic symbols instead of raw func_ addresses. The PR deliberately avoided a shared fighter_OnItemPickup macro because several fighters did not fit the macro/stub pattern. It also removed a large ftCaptain/ftfalcon assembly file, replaced or trimmed several fighter asm snippets, updated per-fighter prototypes, and adjusted fighter.h special-attribute fields needed for matching.
what_changed: Updated the ft_OnItemPickup table in asm/melee/ft/ftdata.s from address-style names such as ftMario_func_800E0A20 and func_800E55A8 to semantic callback names such as ftMario_OnItemPickup, ftFox_OnItemPickup, ftCFalcon_OnItemPickup, ftDonkey_OnItemPickup, ftKirby_OnItemPickup, ftGameWatch_OnItemPickup, ftZakoBoy_OnItemPickup, and ftGKoopa_OnItemPickup.
Updated the ft_OnItemCatch table to reuse the same named OnItemPickup callbacks where applicable, matching the existing comment that Captain's callback is used for both OnItemPickup and OnItemCatch.

Renamed many per-character functions and prototypes from raw func_ symbols to ft<Character>_OnItemPickup, including examples in Captain, Donkey, Dr. Mario, Roy, Falco, Fox, Game & Watch, Ganon, Giga Koopa, Ice Climbers, Koopa, Link, Luigi, Mario, and others.

Added C implementations for several previously-asm or missing item-pickup callbacks, with repeated logic checking func_8026B2B4(ft->x1974_heldItem), switching on func_8026B320(ft->x1974_heldItem), and calling func_80070FB4 with item-kind-dependent parameters.
Changed callback signatures in many files from s32 arg1 to BOOL arg1 where the argument is used as a boolean flag for extra item handling.

Deleted asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCaptain/ftfalcon.s entirely and reduced or relabeled multiple other character asm files, indicating a substantial movement of fighter logic from asm labels into C symbols.

Adjusted src/melee/ft/fighter.h: SpecialAttrs_Kirby x222C changed from u32 to s32, additional Kirby special-attribute fields from 0x2294 through 0x22E8 were added, and SpecialAttrs_Seak x222C changed from u32 to s32.
Updated include/functions.h comment-only unresolved symbol names such as func_8012BAC0 to ftYoshi_8012BAC0 and func_8014A4CC/func_8014A538 to ftGameWatch_8014A4CC/ftGameWatch_8014A538.
smart_moves: Avoided forcing a shared macro where the PR evidence explicitly says an alternate no-macro version was needed; ftdata comments mark Kirby, Link, Purin, Mewtwo, and CLink as cases where the macro did not work, with Mewtwo noted as a stub function.
Kept callback table order intact while only replacing raw function labels with semantic names, making ft_OnItemPickup and ft_OnItemCatch easier to audit by fighter index.

Used consistent local variable ordering and naming in repeated decomp bodies, commonly s32 result, switched_res, unused followed by Fighter* ft = gobj->user_data, which appears to be a matching tactic rather than just style cleanup.
Converted the second callback parameter to BOOL in many prototypes and definitions, preserving the observed behavior of checking arg1 != 0 while documenting the intended meaning.
Preserved known exceptions instead of over-normalizing: table comments explicitly call out non-macro-compatible entries and the Mewtwo stub.
decomp_lessons: For common fighter callback families, semantic table names can be introduced before every individual function is fully understood, as long as the callback table order and prototypes remain correct.
Do not assume a repeated callback is safe to macro-ize; small compiler/matching differences, stubs, or fighter-specific bodies may require explicit functions.
When matching repeated PowerPC switch patterns, temporary variable declarations and their order can matter; the diff repeatedly normalizes to result, switched_res, unused.
Item callback behavior often hinges on held-item helper calls: func_8026B2B4 gates the path, func_8026B320 chooses the case, and func_80070FB4 applies item-state parameters.

Struct signedness and padding in fighter.h can be necessary for unrelated-looking fighter callback matches; here Kirby and Seak special attributes changed from u32 to s32 at x222C, and Kirby received additional fields through x22E8.
Callback tables such as ft_OnItemPickup and ft_OnItemCatch are high-value places to replace func_ labels with meaningful names once the callback role is known.
naming_conventions: Use ft<Character>_OnItemPickup for fighter item-pickup callbacks, e.g. ftMario_OnItemPickup, ftFox_OnItemPickup, ftCFalcon_OnItemPickup, ftDonkey_OnItemPickup, ftGameWatch_OnItemPickup.
Existing project spellings are preserved in symbol names, including ftSeak_OnItemPickup, ftMars_OnItemPickup, ftIcelimber_OnItemPickup, and ftGKoopa_OnItemPickup.
For shared callback use, the semantic name remains OnItemPickup even when the function is also used by ft_OnItemCatch.
Unresolved but partially attributed functions in include/functions.h may be renamed from func_XXXXXXXX comments to ftCharacter_XXXXXXXX comments when fighter ownership is known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed the full ftCaptain/ftfalcon.s assembly file after C-side coverage was sufficient.

Relabeled remaining asm globals for non-decompiled or non-macro-compatible cases, e.g. func_800EE914 became ftKirby_OnItemPickup, func_80144EE0 became ftMewtwo_OnItemPickup, and func_8013C6EC became ftPurin_OnItemPickup.
Used explicit C functions instead of a macro to preserve per-fighter matching differences.

Repeated callback body shape: load Fighter from gobj->user_data, call func_8026B2B4 on x1974_heldItem, if zero switch on func_8026B320, map cases 1/2/3/4 to func_80070FB4(gobj, 1, 1/0/2/3), then conditionally call func_80070C48 or related item visibility/catch helper based on arg1.
Kept Mewtwo as a named stub in asm: ftMewtwo_OnItemPickup is shown as a blr-only function.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace the remaining ft_OnItemPickup table comments saying macro doesn't work.
Search for cleanup or correction of ftIcelimber_OnItemPickup spelling and whether it is intentional project convention.
Check later decomp of Mewtwo item callbacks to see if ftMewtwo_OnItemPickup remains a stub.
Search for PRs that rename func_80070FB4, func_80070C48, func_80070CC4, func_8026B2B4, or func_8026B320 to clarify item callback semantics.
Compare later fighter.h Kirby special-attribute field names for x22C8 through x22E8.
